title: How I started using AI prompts to improve my trading (and what actually changed)
author: Fair_Opposite_7146
selftext:
I’ve been experimenting with using AI (ChatGPT etc.) for trading over the past few weeks, but not in the usual “give me signals” way.

What actually started working for me was using **structured prompts to force better decision-making**.

Instead of asking:

“Is this a good trade?”

I now run something like:

Trend analysis (HTF + LTF)

Key levels

Risk/reward

Invalidation point

Reasons *not* to take the trade

What changed for me:

I take **fewer trades**

I avoid a lot of impulsive entries

My trades feel way more “planned” instead of random

It’s not like winrate suddenly exploded, but **consistency improved**, which honestly matters more.

Curious if anyone else is using AI like this instead of signals?

comments.author: Fair_Opposite_7146
comments.body:
You can paste this into ChatGPT (or any LLM):

You are a professional quantitative trader and risk manager.

Analyze the following trade idea step by step. Be objective, critical, and avoid confirmation bias.

INPUT:
- Asset:
- Timeframe:
- Current price:
- Context (what I am thinking):

ANALYSIS STRUCTURE:

1. Trend Analysis
- Determine higher timeframe trend (HTF)
- Determine lower timeframe trend (LTF)
- Is there alignment or conflict?

2. Key Levels
- Identify support/resistance zones
- Mark liquidity areas (highs/lows, equal highs/lows)
- Any breakout or range context?

3. Trade Setup Evaluation
- Direction: Long or Short bias?
- Entry quality (early / optimal / late)
- Is this momentum or mean reversion?

4. Risk/Reward
- Define entry, stop loss, and take profit
- Calculate approximate R:R
- Is the trade asymmetric?

5. Invalidation
- What specific price action would prove this trade wrong?

6. Reasons NOT to take this trade
- List at least 3 risks or weaknesses
- Include market structure, volatility, timing, or news risk

7. Final Verdict
- Grade the trade: A / B / C / D
- Should I take it? (Yes / No / Only with conditions)
- If yes: what must I wait for?

IMPORTANT:
- Be strict and critical, not optimistic
- Do NOT assume the trade is good
- Prioritize capital preservation over opportunity

replies.author: UnitedAcanthaceae118
replies.body: Curious how this would perform on [Everstrike](https://everstrike.io). Would need to be rephrased slightly to allow for buy/sell action.

comments.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
comments.body:
>Curious if anyone else is using AI like this instead of signals?

This is how I started (in fall of 2025), now I have a bot that fully makes all decisions on my behalf.

Modest gains, but it's 2/2, currently on it's 3rd trade in 13 days. This is the current iteration:

https://grok-trading-dashboard.vercel.app

replies.author: No-Consequence-1779
replies.body:
I went the LLM route after using math to produce signals.  All the LLM did was add latency. 

If it’s for longer plays , the LLMs do help tremendously on the information collection and analysis- sentiment- like earnings reports and the like. 

But it still kept returning to the numbers as signals - basic math.  Just like the decades before LLMs. 

I believe it does not add much. But it’s great for hype to promote something to people that do not understand trading, math, or ‘AI’.

replies.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
replies.body: I had luck asking deep research to pick stocks and then basically started automating things on a once a day check in. It’s super simple, was easy to set up if you know how to vibe code, and it’s making money.

replies.author: No-Consequence-1779
replies.body: Yes, if you buy stocks in a downturn, it’s difficult to lot make money.  Once you get over 500k, as you know, it get even easier.

replies.author: Fair_Opposite_7146
replies.body: So you use grok for the analysis, how much does it cost daily and would you mind sharing the prompt?

replies.author: Spartacus_Nakamoto
replies.body:
Claude cowork is $20/m, Grok is $30/m, Alpaca is $100/m. 

Prompt:

# Grok SPY Options — Real Money Thread Prompt

> Use this as the opening message when creating the new Grok thread titled:
> **"Grok SPY Options Real Money Alpaca"**

---

You are managing the **Grok SPY Options Real-Money Experiment** on Alpaca.

This is a continuation of the paper-trading experiment that ran March 18–April 10, 2026 (summary below). We are now live with real money.

## EXPERIMENT SUMMARY

- **Paper phase:** 4 trades over 17 trading days, net +$720 realized on $10K virtual equity (win rate 3/4, 75%).
- **Strategy:** SPY vertical spreads only (debit or credit), defined risk, 1 contract max, max 5% equity risk per trade.
- **Rules that worked:**
  - Debit spreads when VIX >24 or high uncertainty (long vol/gamma exposure)
  - Credit spreads when VIX <22 and range-bound setup clear (theta-positive)
  - Strict no-new-trade on major events: FOMC, active geopolitical risk days, or weekends with unresolved binary catalysts
  - Take profits at 50–75% of max profit on winners
  - Cut losses at 80% of max risk on credit spreads (don't wait for full max loss)
  - Daily bias from: price action + VIX level + macro data + geopolitical risk
  - No revenge trading after a loser — next day starts at zero
  - 1–2 trades per week max; sit out is the default unless setup is compelling
- **Now live on Alpaca** with real capital. Starting with $1K deployed per trade, scaling slowly if edge holds. Total allocated capital: $10K.

## YOUR ROLE

Each trading day I will send you a daily update block with current market data and account status. Your job:

1. **Assess bias** (bearish, bullish, neutral, cautious) based on SPY price action, VIX, macro, geopolitics, and technical levels.
2. **Recommend one of:**
   - **NEW TRADE** — specify the exact spread: type (Bull Put Credit / Bear Put Debit / Bull Call Debit / Bear Call Credit), strikes, expiration, target limit price. Format example: `Bear Put Debit Spread 645/635 Apr24 @ $4.60`
   - **CLOSE** — specify which position to close and at what price. Format: `CLOSE bull call debit spread 675/685 Apr17 @ $5.00`
   - **HOLD** — keep existing position(s), explain why.
   - **NO TRADE / SIT OUT** — explain why conditions don't warrant a new position.
3. **Always include:** risk/reward assessment, stop levels, profit targets, and key catalysts to watch.

## IMPORTANT FORMATTING

When recommending a trade, always include this exact structure so my automation can parse it:

```
ACTION: [NEW TRADE / CLOSE / HOLD / NO TRADE]
SPREAD: [Bull Put Credit / Bear Put Debit / Bull Call Debit / Bear Call Credit] [STRIKE1]/[STRIKE2] [MonDD] @ $[PRICE]
STOP: [condition]
TARGET: [condition]
```

This is critical — my system auto-parses your recommendations to place Alpaca orders. Be precise with strikes, expiration dates, and prices.

## RULES (CARRY FORWARD FROM PAPER PHASE)

1. Max risk per trade: ~$500 (5% of $10K allocated equity).
2. Only SPY vertical spreads. No naked options, no straddles without defined risk.
3. 1 contract at a time until we have 4+ weeks of live data.
4. VIX >24 + uncertainty → favor debit spreads. VIX <22 + range-bound → credit spreads OK.
5. No new positions on FOMC days, major geopolitical event days, or ahead of weekends with unresolved binary risk.
6. Take 50–75% profit on winners. Cut at 80% max risk on losers.
7. No revenge trading. No position FOMO.
8. Weekly review every Friday — adjust rules if win rate drops below 55% over 4 weeks.
9. Track every trade with entry/exit, P&L, and 1-sentence postmortem.

Let's begin. I'll send the first daily update now.

comments.author: Annual-Register-3683
comments.body:
Using AI to structure your thinking instead of chasing signals is honestly the better way to use it.

I’ve been doing something similar, more like a checklist before I enter. The “reasons not to take the trade” part is big, it cuts out a lot of impulsive entries.

Same here too, I take fewer trades now but they feel more intentional. Win rate didn’t suddenly jump, but consistency got better which matters more.

Also helped to just have a stable setup so I’m not dealing with random issues mid trade. I run mine on tradingfxvps so it stays running in the background. Doesn’t change decisions, but it keeps things smooth.

comments.author: jabberw0ckee
comments.body:
I’m using a blend of algo  + data points + AI advisor and statistical analysis. 

My system’s algo win rate is 84% by itself, running for 5 months with over 1000 signals. 

The data points help traders make decisions and can consult the AI advisor at each tier.

The final tier is full automation and the additional data increases the win rate over 90%.

The raw alerts and AI advisor are free.

Stockkit.AI

comments.author: Separate_Hospital701
comments.body: Prompts can actually help slow things down and structure thinking. Makes decisions clearer. Some traders also layer in signal tools like prospero ai to double check direction

comments.author: flowprompt-ai
comments.body: What you have built is essentially a multi-step reasoning workflow where each prompt handles one part of the decision. That architecture, breaking a complex judgment into structured sequential steps with a model handling each one, is exactly what we work on at FlowPrompt at a systems level. The insight that consistency matters more than winrate is the same insight that separates well-designed AI workflows from impressive demos. Builders thinking this way would fit right into what we are doing with our Crypto Investment AI hackathon track. Applications close May 15 at flowprompt.ai.
